content.full_text:
GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

GORDON GRAY (1877-1967)
W. P. CARY (1882-1943)
WALTER AMES (1893-1980)
FRANK A. FRYE (1904-1970)

1700 FIRST INTERSTATE PLAZA
401 B STREET

OTHER OFFICES
IN
LA JOLLA
EL CENTRO

SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101-4219
TELEPHONE (619) 699-2700
FAX (619) 239-4237
FAX (619) 236-1048
TELEX II 910 335-1273

WRITER'S DIRECT LINE

(619) 699-3644

January 31, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Requirement to File Form 721

Ladies and Gentlemen:

We represent Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management.
Nicholas-Applegate is one of several investment managers retained
by the San Francisco City Employees' Retirement System. The
Retirement System has recently requested that the principals of
Nicholas-Applegate complete and file Form 721, Statement of
Economic Interests, on the basis that individuals who manage
investments for city and county agencies are required to comply
with Government Code Section 87200 as recently amended by
Assembly Bill No. 941.

Nicholas-Applegate is a private limited partnership,
with Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management, Inc. acting as the
general partner. None of the limited partners or employees of
the general partner are officials, candidates or employees of any
state, county or city agency. We contacted the Fair Political
Practices Commission and spoke to Ms. Alice Hughes about why the
individuals who manage the Retirement System's investments are
required to make the same disclosures as public officials and
candidates. She indicated that Assembly Bill No. 941 extended to
private individuals because of the inclusion of the word
"employees." We have reviewed Assembly Bill No. 941 and we can
find no basis for the Commission's extension of the financial
disclosure requirements to private individuals.

Nicholas-Applegate, as an investment adviser registered
with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment
Advisers Act of 1940 and with the securities authorities of
California and over forty other states, is already subject to

GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE

Fair Political Practices Commission
January 31, 1990
Page 2


significant regulatory oversight, including required disclosure
to public agencies and clients. It would be very burdensome to
be required to make additional disclosures in every California
jurisdiction in which Nicholas-Applegate has public agency
clients, and such disclosure will be largely duplicative of what
is already publicly available.

We respectfully request your advice and comment as to
the statutory and/or regulatory basis for the inclusion of
private individuals in the disclosure requirements, since the
only reference requiring such disclosure that we have located is
in the instructions to Form 721 which were published by your
agency.

We have not complied with the January 31, 1990 filing
deadline due to the fact that the Form 721 package was delivered
to Nicholas-Applegate only within the last several days, however,
pending receipt of your response, we will promptly respond
accordingly.

I look forward to hearing from you.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Margaret M. Shea
For
GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE

MMS:k11
cc: Fred C. Applegate
Arthur Nicholas
Cameron Jay Raines, Esq.

208:L981

sections.question_synthetic: Are private investment managers retained by public employee retirement systems required to file Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) under Government Code Section 8200 as amended by Assembly Bill 941?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is embedded in the body of the letter. The letter references prior contact with FPPC staff (Ms. Alice Hughes) and requests advice regarding Form 721 filing requirements. Filing deadline was January 31, 1990, and compliance was deferred pending FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Are private investment managers retained by public employee retirement systems required to file Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) under Government Code Section 8200 as amended by Assembly Bill 941?

Nicholas-Applegate is one of several investment managers retained by the San Francisco City Employees' Retirement System. The Retirement System has recently requested that the principals of Nicholas-Applegate complete and file Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests, on the basis that individuals who manage investments for city and county agencies are required to comply with Government Code Section 87200 as recently amended by Assembly Bill No. 941. Nicholas-Applegate is a private limited partnership, with Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management, Inc. acting as the general partner. None of the limited partners or employees of the general partner are officials, candidates or employees of any state, county or city agency. We contacted the Fair Political Practices Commission and spoke to Ms. Alice Hughes about why the individuals who manage the Retirement System's investments are required to make the same disclosures as public officials and candidates. She indicated that Assembly Bill No. 941 extended to private individuals because of the inclusion of the word "employees." We have reviewed Assembly Bill No. 941 and we can find no basis for the Commission's extension of the financial disclosure requirements to private individuals. Nicholas-Applegate, as an investment adviser registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and with the securities authorities of California and over forty other states, is already subject to GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE Fair Political Practices Commission January 31, 1990 Page 2 significant regulatory oversight, including required disclosure to public agencies and clients. It would be very burdensome to be required to make additional disclosures in every California jurisdiction in which Nicholas-Applegate has public agency clients, and such disclosure will be largely duplicative of what is already publicly available. We respectfully request your advice and comment as to the statutory and/or regulatory basis for the inclusion of private individuals in the disclosure requirements, since the only reference requiring such disclosure that we have located is in the instructions to Form 721 which were published by your agency. We have not complied with the January 31, 1990 filing deadline due to the fact that the Form 721 package was delivered to Nicholas-Applegate only within the last several days, however, pending receipt of your response, we will promptly respond accordingly. I look forward to hearing from you. Very truly yours, [signature] Margaret M. Shea For GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE MMS:k11 cc: Fred C. Applegate Arthur Nicholas Cameron Jay Raines, Esq. 208:L981
embedding.summary: Law firm Gray, Cary, Ames & Frye requests FPPC guidance on whether Nicholas-Applegate Capital Management, a private investment adviser, must file Form 721 disclosures based on managing investments for the San Francisco City Employees' Retirement System. The firm argues that private individuals should not be subject to these requirements and seeks clarification of the statutory basis for including private managers in the disclosure requirements.
